description_zh: 李维斯（Levi's）是全球最知名的牛仔裤品牌，1853年由巴伐利亚移民李维·斯特劳斯在旧金山创立。1873年，李维·斯特劳斯与裁缝雅各布·戴维斯合作获得了铆钉加固工作裤的专利——这被公认为现代牛仔裤的诞生。Levi's 501经典直筒牛仔裤自问世以来已畅销超过一个世纪，成为美国文化的重要标志之一。品牌的橙色标签、双弧线后袋缝线和红标「Levi's」Logo具有极高的视觉识别度。Levi's不仅是牛仔裤的发明者，更定义了美国西部开拓精神、摇滚反叛文化和青年自由态度。从马龙·白兰度到詹姆斯·迪恩，从苹果创始人到当代街头潮人，Levi's跨越了所有阶层和世代。如今品牌产品涵盖牛仔服饰、上衣、外套及配饰，在全球110多个国家销售，年营收超60亿美元。

languages.en: Levi's is the world's most iconic denim brand, founded in 1853 by Bavarian immigrant Levi Strauss in San Francisco. In 1873, Levi Strauss partnered with tailor Jacob Davis to patent rivet-reinforced work pants — widely recognized as the birth of modern blue jeans. The Levi's 501 classic straight-leg jean has been a bestseller for over a century, becoming a vital symbol of American culture. The brand's orange tab, double-arc back pocket stitching, and red 'Levi's' label are instantly recognizable worldwide. Levi's didn't just invent jeans — it defined the American frontier spirit, rock and roll rebellion, and youthful freedom. From Marlon Brando to James Dean, from Apple founders to contemporary streetwear enthusiasts, Levi's transcends all classes and generations. Today the brand spans denim, tops, outerwear, and accessories, sold in over 110 countries with annual revenue exceeding $6 billion.
